question: Which marketing orientation assumes consumers will favor products that are widely available and low in cost?
A company focuses on improving product quality and features because it believes the best product will win. This reflects the:
The marketing concept is based primarily on the idea that firms should:

answers.B: Create superior customer value for chosen target markets
answers.C: Lower production costs at any cost

question: Which statement best captures customer value?

answers.A: Benefits received relative to costs given up
answers.B: The seller’s production cost plus markup
answers.C: The lowest price in the market

question: Customer equity refers to:

answers.A: The total combined customer lifetime values of all the firm’s customers
answers.B: The firm’s market share in one quarter
answers.D: The gap between list price and sale price

question: A firm spends heavily to close one sale but ignores post-purchase service. From a marketing perspective, the main flaw is failing to build:
Which of the following is the clearest example of needs, wants, and demands in the correct order?

answers.A: Phone, iPhone, willingness and ability to buy the iPhone

question: A retailer donates part of sales to environmental causes while still aiming to satisfy customers profitably. This most closely fits the:
Which option is part of a complete modern marketing process?

answers.A: Understand the marketplace, create value, build relationships, capture value
answers.B: Produce first, discount later, advertise last
answers.C: Maximize output, minimize service, avoid customization

question: A SWOT analysis classifies a competitor’s aggressive price cuts as a:
An aging population affecting demand patterns is part of the ______ environment.
A recession that reduces consumers’ purchasing power belongs to the ______ environment.
New privacy legislation restricting data collection is part of the:
A firm’s mission statement should primarily:

answers.A: State quarterly revenue targets in detail
answers.B: Define the business in terms of customer needs served

question: In the BCG matrix, a product in a high-growth market with low relative market share is a:
The best definition of strategic planning is:

answers.B: Developing and maintaining a strategic fit between goals, capabilities, and changing opportunities

question: A company’s suppliers, marketing intermediaries, customers, competitors, and publics are part of the:
Market segmentation means:

answers.A: Splitting a market into distinct groups with different needs or responses

question: Which is NOT one of the major bases for consumer market segmentation?
A segment should be measurable, accessible, substantial, differentiable, and:
Target marketing involves evaluating segments and then:

answers.B: Selecting one or more segments to enter

question: Positioning is best defined as:

answers.B: Designing the company’s offering and image to occupy a distinct place in target customers’ minds

question: A firm markets the same basic product to the whole market with one offer. This is:
A company sells separate versions of cereal for kids, athletes, and health-conscious adults. This is:
A startup with limited resources chooses one narrow segment and serves it extremely well. This is:
Perceptual mapping is most useful for understanding:

answers.B: How consumers view competing brands on key dimensions

question: A product’s ability to be modified to suit individual users is called:
The length of a product line refers to:

answers.A: The number of versions within the line
answers.B: The number of product lines carried

question: Adding more items within the present range of a product line is:
A company introduces a cheaper version below its existing mid-priced offering. This is:
A company adds more premium versions above its current range to reach upscale buyers. This is:
The adoption process begins when a consumer first becomes aware of an innovation. This stage is:
A new app offers a free trial so customers can experiment before committing. This most directly improves:
Which characteristic of innovations means the degree to which an innovation is difficult to understand or use?
Which group in the diffusion process adopts new ideas just before the average person?
A new product launches at a high initial price to skim revenues from segments willing to pay more. This is:
A firm sets price by taking unit cost and adding a standard markup. This is:
Value-based pricing starts with:
Penetration pricing is most appropriate when:

answers.A: Demand is highly price inelastic and competitors cannot enter
answers.B: The goal is to enter the market quickly and attract many buyers
answers.C: The product is highly differentiated and protected from imitation
answers.D: The firm sells a main product with required accessories

question: Derived demand in B2B markets means:

answers.A: Business demand comes from demand for consumer goods
answers.B: Business demand is random and trend-driven
answers.C: Business demand is always highly elastic

question: A razor is priced low, but replacement blades are priced high. This is:
A streaming service offers basic, standard, and premium tiers with different feature bundles. This is mainly:
Target costing begins with an ideal selling price based on customer value and then:

answers.B: Backs out a target cost that ensures desired profit

question: Which statement about B2B demand is most accurate?

answers.A: It is usually derived and often fluctuates more than consumer demand
answers.B: It is independent of final consumer demand
answers.C: It depends mainly on social media trends
answers.D: It is always less volatile than consumer demand

question: A marketing channel is best described as:

answers.B: A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product available to users

question: Using more than one but fewer than all willing intermediaries in a market is:
Luxury watches sold through only one authorized dealer in a city are using:
Snack brands that try to be available in as many outlets as possible rely on:
Wholesalers primarily add value by:

answers.C: Buying in large quantities and reselling to retailers or business users

question: Which flow in a channel goes from buyers back to sellers?
A corporate VMS is one in which:

answers.B: One channel member owns multiple levels of production or distribution

question: Franchising is most closely associated with which type of vertical marketing system?
Which logistics goal best reflects integrated supply chain management?

answers.A: Maximize warehouse space regardless of cost
answers.B: Provide target customer service at the lowest total cost

question: The promotion mix consists of advertising, sales promotion, public relations, personal selling, and:
Paid, nonpersonal presentation and promotion of ideas, goods, or services by an identified sponsor is:
Short-term incentives to encourage purchase or sale are known as:
Building good relations with the company’s various publics by obtaining favorable publicity and handling rumors or events is:
Two-way, personal interaction between salespeople and individual customers is:
In the communication process, the reaction the receiver sends back to the sender is:
Which response hierarchy model is summarized by awareness, knowledge, liking, preference, conviction, and purchase?
Using the same core message and coordinated media across channels so the brand speaks with one voice reflects:
Setting a promotion budget based on what the company thinks it can spare is the:
In push strategy, the producer directs marketing activities primarily toward:

answers.A: Final consumers to pull the product through the channel
answers.B: Channel members to induce them to carry and promote the product

question: In a pull strategy, promotion is aimed mainly at:

answers.C: Final consumers to create demand that pulls the product through channels

question: A company gets retailers excited with trade allowances and sales force pressure so they stock a new brand. This is mainly:
Which statement best captures word-of-mouth influence?

answers.A: It matters only for low-involvement goods
answers.B: Consumers trust company ads more than other consumers
answers.C: People often rely heavily on others’ opinions and recommendations
answers.D: It is irrelevant in digital environments

question: A company paying influencers to showcase a product is using a modern form of:
The most likely benefit of direct and digital marketing compared with traditional mass communication is:

answers.C: More immediate, interactive, and targeted relationships

question: A coupon delivered to a user after they abandoned an online cart is best classified as:
Which metric is most directly tied to digital campaign efficiency?
